calendar.matters:
PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Jun-07-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Dobrov for (trustee) James Vorisses. Aristotle Marks in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that this is an amended petition for successor trustee, that there is a question of bonding and that it has only seen 2 of the 3 required waivers. Mr. Dobrov stated that the third was filed on Friday and that it could provide a non file stamped copy to the court. The clerk confirmed that a waiver was filed on Friday but further review indicated that it was not the correct waiver. Mr. Dobrov presented the waiver that should have been filed on Friday. The Court addressed Mr. Marks stating that the issue is whether the new trustee for his trust would be required to post bond to protect his trust. Mr. Marks stated that he wants to waive bond. The Court confirmed that Mr. Marks does not want an opportunity to seek advice from independant counsel. The Court stated that based on the representation and the review of the not yet filed waiver that John has signed it will take the petition under submission and if it does not receive John's waiver it will grant the petition with a bond requirement. The petition for substituted judgment trust was taken under submission as of June 7, 2016 allowing time for the waiver to be filed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar May-17-2016 Continued To May-31-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Mar-29-2016 Continued To May-17-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Dobrov for (petitioner) James Vorisses.

The Court stated that it is looking for an amendment or an alternate solution. Mr. Dobrov reported that he had been looking for a trustee and found one, that he had requested a continuance for this date and that he anticipates amending the petition to ask for a different party to serve. The petition re substituted judgment was continued to May 17, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an amended petition to be filed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Feb-16-2016 Continued To Mar-29-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Dobrov for (petitioner/trustee) James Vorisses.

The Court stated that there are a few issues, that problems arise regarding 7.2 & 7.4 barring the children from serving as trustee, that there is nothing in the petition or order that says what change is requested and asked if there was anything to tell us why the trustor went to such pains to exclude the children from serving. Mr Dobrov stated that he does not know, that the trustee has worked with the family for years, that the family does not get along and that they believe that the trustor was referring to the revocable trust not the special needs trust. The Court stated that there is nothing in the trust that provides for the trustee of the special needs trust other than designated and asked if it is consistent with the purposes of the trust to allow someone else to serve. Mr. Dobrov stated that the special needs trust is not large and a professional would not likely take it and that these two brothers do get along. The Court asked if efforts have been made to get someone other than one of the children to serve. Mr. Dobrov stated that they have not looked into that due to the small size of the trust. The Court stated that it will continue the matter to give the trustee time to amend the petition to state specifically how the trust is to be amended and to re-serve it and to look into whether there is a qualified person to serve - possibly another family member or close friend. Mr. Dobrov asked if they find a qualifying person would a bond be required and that court stated that it expects that it would. The petition re substituted judgment trust was continued to March 29, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
